pi_items.source: GeoServer 2.25.2 documentation confirms XML files stored in layouts/ subdirectory of data directory (https://docs.geoserver.org/maintain/en/user/services/wms/decoration.html)
Task description and metadata align with Natural Earth's standard copyright attribution (https://www.naturalearthdata.com/)
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 10) and export_result.sh (line 12) - host path where undecorated map image is saved
Verified in setup_task.sh (line 11) and export_result.sh (line 13) - host path where decorated map image is saved

GeoServer 2.25.2 is a real release from June 18, 2024, published on https://geoserver.org/announcements/vulnerability/2024/06/18/geoserver-2-25-2-released.html; kartoza/geoserver:2.25.2 is a public Docker image on Docker Hub
kartoza/postgis:15-3.3 is a public Docker image on Docker Hub (https://hub.docker.com/r/kartoza/postgis/); tagging convention: first number is PostgreSQL version, second is PostGIS version

pi_items.verified_value: Real public domain data: ne_110m_admin_0_countries, ne_110m_populated_places, ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines, ne_110m_lakes
pi_items.source: Verified from https://www.naturalearthdata.com/ and GitHub (https://github.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector); these datasets are available at 110m scale from Natural Earth CDN (naciscdn.org) and AWS mirror

pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth data is public domain and available from multiple sources: naciscdn.org/naturalearth/ (primary), naturalearth.s3.amazonaws.com/ (AWS), and github.com/nvkelso/natural-earth-vector
pi_items.source: Natural Earth is a free, public domain map dataset maintained by volunteers and supported by NACIS (https://www.naturalearthdata.com/)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses GeoServer 2.25.2 (official release from June 18, 2024) running in a Docker container (kartoza/geoserver:2.25.2) with PostgreSQL 15 and PostGIS 3.3 backend. The task creates a WMS decoration layout file (report_map.xml) in the GeoServer data directory at layouts/report_map.xml. The layout includes scaleline and text decorations with the copyright attribution '© Natural Earth Data'. The task generates two map images from the 'ne:ne_countries' layer (1:110m Natural Earth admin boundaries data, EPSG:4326, 800x400 pixels) - one undecorated (/home/ga/map_undecorated.png) and one with the decoration layout applied (/home/ga/map_decorated.png). All geospatial data is from Natural Earth, a real public domain dataset available from naciscdn.org, AWS, and GitHub. The Natural Earth data includes: ne_110m_admin_0_countries (258 world countries), ne_110m_populated_places (major cities), ne_110m_rivers_lake_centerlines (river drainages and lake centerlines), and ne_110m_lakes. All software versions and data sources are real and publicly available.
